# Known bare `panic!(...)` call sites in contract release code, as of this snapshot.
# Generated by `python3 scripts/check_no_panic.py --update-baseline`.
# Do NOT add to this file to silence a new panic! -- fix the panic!
# instead (use panic_with_error! with a typed ContractError variant).
# This file only exists to grandfather pre-existing debt; it should
# shrink over time, never grow casually.
contracts/credence_bond/src/claims.rs::panic!("claim amount must be positive")
contracts/credence_bond/src/claims.rs::panic!("claim not found"))
contracts/credence_bond/src/claims.rs::panic!("no pending claims")

contracts/credence_bond/src/emergency_drain.rs::panic!("drain record not found"))
contracts/credence_bond/src/emergency_drain.rs::panic!("recipient must be treasury")

contracts/credence_bond/src/validation.rs::panic!( "bond amount exceeds maximum allowed: {} (maximum: {})", amount, MAX_BOND_AMOUNT )
contracts/credence_bond/src/validation.rs::panic!("bond amount cannot be negative")
contracts/credence_bond/src/validation.rs::panic!("bond duration too long: maximum is 31536000 seconds (365 days)")
contracts/credence_bond/src/validation.rs::panic!("bond duration too short: minimum is 86400 seconds (1 day)")
contracts/credence_bond/src/validation.rs::panic!("recipient cannot be the contract itself")

contracts/credence_bond/src/weighted_attestation.rs::panic!("multiplier_bps exceeds maximum")
contracts/credence_bond/src/weighted_attestation.rs::panic!("stake cannot be negative")
